skill.name: maxhub-youtube
skill.description: YouTube data query assistant. Supports Web/V2 dual APIs covering video details, channel data, search, comments, subtitles, and Shorts.
skill.summary: Query YouTube data including videos, channels, search results, comments, and subtitles through the MaxHub API.
skill.author: XieWxx

security_audit.summary: All 815 static findings are false positives from Markdown documentation formatting. The skill is a legitimate YouTube API client that uses curl commands with a MAXHUB_API_KEY to query the MaxHub API service at aconfig.cn. No malicious code, obfuscated payloads, or unauthorized data exfiltration was detected.

low_findings.title: Markdown backtick formatting misidentified as shell execution
low_findings.description: 586 static findings for Ruby/shell backtick execution are all FALSE POSITIVES. The scanner misidentified Markdown inline code formatting (backtick pairs for code highlighting) as Ruby shell command execution. Example: `https://www.aconfig.cn` is Markdown-formatted URL text, not a shell command. No actual Ruby backtick execution exists in this skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All markdown files use backtick for inline code formatting (e.g., `Base URL: https://...`). No executable Ruby code exists in the skill.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm detection is markdown table formatting
low_findings.description: All 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The scanner detected pipe characters `|` in Markdown tables and MD references in API parameter docs as cryptographic algorithm signatures. These are parameter documentation tables (e.g., `| Parameter | Type | Required |`) not crypto code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Files contain markdown table syntax and parameter documentation, not cryptographic algorithm code.

low_findings.title: Base64 continuation token misidentified as C2 payload
low_findings.description: The 'C2 keywords' finding at api-video.md:934 is a FALSE POSITIVE. The base64-encoded string is a YouTube continuation token for comment reply pagination (reply_continuation_token). It is a legitimate API pagination parameter, not a C2 communication payload.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The string is documented as a continuation token in the API reference, surrounded by pagination parameter documentation.

low_findings.title: High file entropy from Chinese text and base64 tokens
low_findings.description: Heuristic entropy findings for files with Chinese characters and base64 API tokens are FALSE POSITIVES. SKILL.md contains Chinese/English bilingual content which has higher entropy than plain English. api-channel.md and api-video.md contain base64-encoded continuation tokens which raise file entropy.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Files contain bilingual Chinese/English content which naturally has higher character entropy. Base64 continuation tokens are legitimate API pagination data.

low_findings.title: System reconnaissance on public YouTube channel IDs
low_findings.description: Static findings for 'System reconnaissance' are FALSE POSITIVES. The detected strings are example YouTube channel IDs (e.g., UCkRfArvrzheW2E7b6SVT7vQ) used in API documentation examples. These are publicly available channel identifiers, not system reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The detected strings are YouTube channel IDs used as API parameter examples, which are public identifiers by design.

low_findings.title: Expected network calls to MaxHub API service
low_findings.description: 65 network findings for hardcoded URLs to aconfig.cn are TRUE POSITIVES but expected behavior. The skill calls the MaxHub API (www.aconfig.cn) to fetch YouTube data. All URLs point to a single, legitimate third-party API service with documented endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network calls are the core functionality of this API client skill. Only one legitimate API domain is referenced. No suspicious or unknown endpoints detected.

low_findings.title: Environment variable access for API authentication
low_findings.description: 17 env_access findings for MAXHUB_API_KEY are TRUE POSITIVES but expected. The skill requires a MAXHUB_API_KEY environment variable for Bearer token authentication. This is standard API client behavior, clearly documented as required.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Environment variable access is for API authentication via Bearer token. The skill clearly documents this requirement and does not exfiltrate the key.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell command execution via curl for API calls
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs the AI to execute curl commands with shell backtick syntax to call the MaxHub API. While this is expected for an API client, the AI executes these commands with network access and credential access simultaneously.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: SKILL.md explicitly shows curl commands with Authorization header using environment variable. This is the skill's designed behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Combined network + command execution + credential access
dangerous_patterns.description: The critical heuristic finding about the combination of code execution, network access, and credential access is TRUE but expected. The skill sends curl requests with API keys to an external API - this is standard for API client skills.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The skill design intentionally combines these capabilities to function as an API client. No evidence of abuse or exfiltration to unauthorized destinations.

content.user_title: Query YouTube data for research and analysis
content.value_statement: YouTube data is hard to access through standard interfaces. This skill lets you query video details, channel information, search results, comments, and subtitles using a simple API key.
actual_capabilities: Query YouTube video details including views, likes, description, and subtitles
Search for videos and channels with advanced filtering options
Retrieve channel statistics and full video listings
Collect video comments and reply threads for analysis
Access trending videos and Shorts data from different regions
Auto-detect language and respond in Chinese or English
limitations: Requires a valid MAXHUB_API_KEY from aconfig.cn to function
Data comes from third-party APIs and may not reflect real-time YouTube data
Rate limits apply based on your API key tier
Does not support video upload or channel management operations

use_cases.title: Competitor channel research for content creators
use_cases.description: Analyze competitor YouTube channels by searching, comparing video performance, and identifying content strategy patterns.
use_cases.target_user: Content creators and social media managers

use_cases.title: Video SEO optimization research
use_cases.description: Study top-ranking videos for target keywords and extract SEO patterns from titles, tags, and descriptions.

use_cases.title: Audience sentiment and feedback analysis
use_cases.description: Collect video comments and analyze viewer sentiment, questions, and feedback for product or content improvement.

prompt_templates.title: Find recent videos by keyword
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for videos about Python programming from the last month. Show me the top 10 results with view counts and channel names.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner user searching for video content

prompt_templates.title: Channel performance overview
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the channel @TechWithTim. Show me their subscriber count, total videos, and a summary of their top 10 videos by views.
prompt_templates.scenario: Content researcher evaluating a YouTube channel

prompt_templates.title: Multi-channel comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare the YouTube channels of Tesla, Ford, and GM. Show me subscriber counts, upload frequency, and their most popular video types.

prompt_templates.title: Deep analysis with comments
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for machine learning tutorial, get the top 10 videos, fetch their comments, and tell me what topics viewers ask about most.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user performing comprehensive video and comment analysis

output_examples.input: Show me trending videos in the United States today
output_examples.output:
Top 10 trending videos in the US:
1. [Title] by ChannelName - 2.5M views
2. [Title] by ChannelName - 1.8M views
...

Summary: Gaming content leads today, followed by tech reviews.

output_examples.input: Get channel info for UCXuqSBlHAE6Xw-yeJA0Tunw and list their recent videos
output_examples.output:
Channel: Tech Reviews Weekly
Subscribers: 1.2M
Total videos: 342

Recent videos:
1. [Title] - 2 days ago - 120K views
2. [Title] - 5 days ago - 85K views
...

best_practices: Set your MAXHUB_API_KEY before using the skill to avoid authentication errors
Start with broad searches and use specific filters to refine results
Run independent queries in parallel when comparing multiple channels
anti_patterns: Do not share your API key in conversation output or prompts
Do not ask for raw API response data - let the skill format results naturally
Do not use this skill to download copyrighted video content

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need a MAXHUB_API_KEY from www.aconfig.cn to use this skill.

faq.question: Is this an official YouTube API tool?
faq.answer: No, this skill uses third-party MaxHub APIs to access YouTube data.

faq.question: Can I get real-time YouTube data?
faq.answer: Data from the API provider may be cached and can have a short delay.

faq.question: What languages does the skill support?
faq.answer: It supports Chinese and English. It detects your language and responds in the same language.

faq.question: Can I export the data to a file?
faq.answer: Results appear in the chat. You can copy data manually for external use.

faq.question: Does this work with any AI tool?
faq.answer: Yes, it works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
